title: store auth to authfile file if 'nokeyring' specified
body:
This PR allows the value of --authfile to be "nokeyring".
Returns empty string if both --authfile and REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE are not set.
Returns filepath if one of --authfile or REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE is set to a filepath. If --authfile and REGISTRY_AUTH_FILE are filepath, we respect --authfile

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: Imagine we want to add a third credential store. I wouldn't know how we should expose that in the CLI as we already started embedding the knowledge into `--authfile`.

> I feel rather strongly against that as I think we're mixing apples and oranges into the `--authfile`. If we want to turn off using the keyring, then we should add dedicated flag to it and pass that knowledge down to c/image via the SystemContext.

+1.  Let's make it clearer they are different things.

Ok, so we have 
--auth-store = keyring|file 
And --auth-store=keyring conflicts with --auth-file != ""

> Ok, so we have
> --auth-store = keyring|file
> And --auth-store=keyring conflicts with --auth-file != ""

Currently it's enough to set `--auth-file` to disable using the keyring (which uses the auth files as a fall back). If we want to extend this, I propose moving the discussion to c/image and we need @mtrmac's pair of eyes on it.

The issue is setting a good default if not using keyring.  Not really something we need to have @mtrmac for.  
Bottom line, I believe the most secure is kernel keyring, second most secure is keyring in XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, so that it is stored in TMPFS.  

Forcing users to specify path to keyring, seems like the best we can do, since most users will never specify this, it is probably fine, to just remove code on XDG_RUNTIME_DIR.

Just check if user specified --auth-file or REGISTRY_AUTHFILE is set, otherwise we pass in "" causing containers/image to use keyring.

As far as c/image goes, I agree with @vrothberg and I would pretty strongly resist special-casing (case-insensitive, even!) value of the `SystemContext.AuthFilePath` parameter. Adding extra well-typed fields with strict semantics is very cheap; overloading conflicting semantics onto existing fields (`nokeyring` is a perfectly valid file name, after all) is much more expensive longer-term.

---

WRT the idea of explicitly disabling keyring use (`--auth-file=nokeyring` or `--auth-store=file`), it’s a bit ugly to implement that by buildah hard-coding the explicit `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` logic. What if we wanted to change the default in c/image again?

So, I guess that ideally we would want to give callers explicit control (which was already possible to anticipate in https://github.com/containers/image/issues/662 ) , though if buildah is happy to hard-code that path and maintain that copy of the code, sure, it can.
Closing this since this needs a full rework.
